meta.purpose: QA review of NORS coding validation handoff; preserve confirmed keyword improvements and block conflicting F-code recommendations
meta.normalization_note: The incoming validation handoff included useful ambiguity guidance but also conflicted with ACL/NORC Table 2 on several F-code labels. This batch keeps only production-safe routing corrections aligned with the app's current NORS hierarchy and ACL Table 2.

source_verification_rows.source_name: ACL/NORC NORS Table 2 Complaint Codes and Definitions
source_verification_rows.preferred_use: Production source of truth for NORS complaint-code labels, definitions, examples, and reporting tips.
source_verification_rows.limitations: Expiration date shown in the PDF is 10/31/2024; recheck ACL/NORC for any later approved Table 2 before major production releases.

source_verification_rows.preferred_use: Quick-reference confirmation of current NORS minor-code labels, including F02, F08, F12, F13, H01, and H02.
source_verification_rows.limitations: Use the full ACL/NORC Table 2 PDF for definitions and examples when there is any ambiguity.

source_conflict_rows.issue: Incoming handoff mislabeled several current NORS F-codes.
source_conflict_rows.source_a: ACL/NORC Table 2 identifies F06 as Access to health-related services, F08 as Incontinence care, F12 as Chemical restraint, and H01/H02 as Dietary codes.
source_conflict_rows.source_b: Incoming validation handoff suggested F06 for toileting/incontinence, F07/F08 for food/dining, and F12 for medication errors.
source_conflict_rows.conflict_description: Those handoff recommendations conflict with the current app hierarchy and official Table 2. Promoting them would corrupt production code routing.
source_conflict_rows.recommended_resolution: Keep the app's current NORS hierarchy as production truth. Route call-light response to F02, incontinence care to F08, medication administration/errors to F04, chemical restraint to F12, food service to H01, and dining/hydration to H02.
source_conflict_rows.human_review_note: Do not import the conflicting F-code labels from the handoff. Use this row as a QA record explaining why those recommendations were rejected.

source_conflict_rows.issue: Generic call-light phrases can over-trigger equipment or accident mappings.
source_conflict_rows.source_a: ACL/NORC Table 2 F02 explicitly covers failure to promptly respond to call lights/call bells or requests for assistance.
source_conflict_rows.source_b: Older keyword rows associated generic 'call light' with accident prevention, symptoms unattended, and assistive devices.
source_conflict_rows.conflict_description: Broad call-light matches caused noisy results, including F09 assistive devices, when the user intended staff response delay.
source_conflict_rows.recommended_resolution: Use F02 as the default for generic call light/call bell response. Use F09 only when the device itself is broken, not working, inaccessible, or missing.
source_conflict_rows.human_review_note: The shared crosswalk logic now suppresses older broad call-light rows from accident/equipment knowledge rows.

keyword_routing_rows.phrase_pattern: left wet|left in bed wet|left in bed soaked|left in wet brief|left in soiled brief|no one changed me|diaper not changed|incontinence care not provided
keyword_routing_rows.explanation_for_trace: ACL Table 2 places incontinence and catheter care under F08. If the concern also describes a request for help that went unanswered, F02 is a related response-to-assistance code.
keyword_routing_rows.warning_or_human_review_note: Consider A05 only when the facts suggest severe essential-care failure that threatens health, safety, or welfare.

keyword_routing_rows.phrase_pattern: wrong pill|wrong dose|wrong medication|medication error|missed dose|missed med|gave me someone else's medicine|late insulin|med pass skipped
keyword_routing_rows.explanation_for_trace: ACL Table 2 places medication given in error, not given on time, missed, expired, mislabeled, or undocumented medication under F04 Medications. F12 is chemical restraint, not the general medication-error code.
keyword_routing_rows.warning_or_human_review_note: If the medication issue involves drugs used for discipline or convenience rather than to treat symptoms, review F12 Chemical restraint separately.

keyword_routing_rows.phrase_pattern: chemical restraint|drugged for staff convenience|sedated for convenience|psychotropic for discipline|unnecessary antipsychotic restraint
keyword_routing_rows.explanation_for_trace: ACL Table 2 defines F12 as Chemical restraint: medication used for discipline or convenience that is not required to treat medical symptoms.
keyword_routing_rows.warning_or_human_review_note: Separate chemical-restraint concerns from ordinary medication administration errors, which route to F04.

keyword_routing_rows.phrase_pattern: food is cold|food cold|food tastes bad|same food every day|tiny portions|menu not followed|no alternatives|snacks unavailable
keyword_routing_rows.explanation_for_trace: ACL Table 2 places food quantity, quality, variety, choice, temperature, and meal/snack timing under H01 Food services.
keyword_routing_rows.warning_or_human_review_note: Use A05 only for willful or severe food deprivation that meets gross-neglect criteria.

keyword_routing_rows.phrase_pattern: tray left out of reach|no one helped me eat|no one helped him eat|not enough to drink|dehydrated|never opened milk carton|needs feeding help
keyword_routing_rows.explanation_for_trace: ACL Table 2 places dining assistance, tray setup, adaptive equipment for eating, direct feeding assistance, fluids, and hydration reminders under H02 Dining and hydration.
keyword_routing_rows.warning_or_human_review_note: If the facts suggest willful deprivation or serious risk from lack of food/fluid assistance, flag for human review.

keyword_routing_rows.phrase_pattern: I want a different doctor|won't let me keep my doctor|can't choose doctor|forced pharmacy|can't choose hospice|therapy schedule not my choice|med time not consistent with my routine
keyword_routing_rows.explanation_for_trace: ACL Table 2 places denial of the right to choose a physician, pharmacy, hospice, or preferred treatment schedule under D01 Choice in health care.
keyword_routing_rows.warning_or_human_review_note: Do not route provider-choice language to medication error unless wrong drug, dose, time, omission, or documentation facts are present.

keyword_routing_rows.phrase_pattern: after I complained|because I complained|after we complained|because we complained|punished for complaining|retaliation|retaliated against me|things got worse after I complained
keyword_routing_rows.explanation_for_trace: ACL/NORC Table 2 places acts of retaliation or revenge by facility staff after a complaint under D06. The examples include threat of discharge, lack of care, requests ignored, call lights unanswered, and rough handling when those acts occur in response to a resident or family complaint.
keyword_routing_rows.warning_or_human_review_note: When retaliation language is present, review D06 as a distinct rights complaint. Also consider the underlying harm as a separate complaint when the case facts support it, such as F02 for ignored call lights or C03 for retaliatory discharge threats.

tooltip_rows.label: Current NORS Code Set
tooltip_rows.short_help: Use the current ACL/NORC Table 2 code labels before importing outside summaries.
tooltip_rows.details: The QA handoff included useful ambiguity guidance but mislabeled several current F-codes. Production routing should keep F02 for call-light response, F08 for incontinence care, F12 for chemical restraint, H01 for food service, and H02 for dining/hydration.

human_review_flags.issue: Do not import handoff rows that relabel F06, F07, F08, F12, H01, or H02 contrary to ACL/NORC Table 2.
human_review_flags.recommended_action: Keep the current app hierarchy. Use F02 for response to assistance, F08 for incontinence care, F04 for medication administration/error issues, F12 for chemical restraint, H01 for food service, and H02 for dining/hydration.

human_review_flags.issue: Recheck ACL/NORC Table 2 after 10/31/2024 before locking a final production release.
human_review_flags.recommended_action: The current official PDF used here has an expiration date of 10/31/2024. Before formal release, confirm whether ACL/NORC has published a later Table 2 and update the code hierarchy only if a newer official source changes labels or definitions.
